title: Error-reporting opt-out
why_it_matters: Claude Code sends error reports to Anthropic only when every one of these holds: the user signs in with a Claude Pro or Max subscription, is running Claude Code v2.1.198 or later, is connecting directly to the Claude API, and the organization has no zero-data-retention or HIPAA agreement — error reporting is off in every other case, including every alternative model-provider integration (Google Cloud's Agent Platform, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS, each gated behind its own opt-in environment variable). Anthropic's documentation states known patterns of secrets, file paths, email addresses, and other personal information are redacted before anything leaves the machine. Setting the environment variable DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING=1 opts out regardless of the conditions above. Anthropic's settings reference documents no dedicated boolean settings.json key for this control; DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is documented only as an environment variable, and the same reference states that any environment variable may be checked into settings.json's user-scope env object — so this rule observes DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING only as embedded at that path (env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING) inside the inspected user-level settings.json. It cannot observe an ambient shell- or process-level DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING set outside this file, and it does not evaluate the separate DISABLE_TELEMETRY, DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY, or CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC controls.

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level settings.json sets env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING = "1", opting out of Claude Code's error reporting.

outcomes.when: env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective error-reporting posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers and the ambient process environment may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is unset in the user-level config; error reporting is on only when the inspected install signs in with a Claude Pro or Max subscription, runs Claude Code v2.1.198 or later, connects directly to the Claude API, and the organization has no zero-data-retention or HIPAA agreement — this rule does not determine account, version, or provider state, and uninspected managed, project, local, or CLI-flag layers plus the ambient shell environment may also set DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING outside this file.

outcomes.when: env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1"
outcomes.message: env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1" — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1" — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Data usage
sources.notes: States: 'Claude Code redacts known patterns of secrets, file paths, email addresses, and other personal information before anything leaves your machine. Set DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING=1 to opt out. Error reporting is on only when all of these apply: you sign in with a Claude Pro or Max subscription, you're running Claude Code v2.1.198 or later, you're connecting directly to the Claude API, your organization doesn't have a zero data retention or HIPAA agreement.' The 'Default behaviors by API provider' table documents error reports as: Claude API — on for Pro/Max sign-ins on v2.1.198+, otherwise off, DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING=1 to disable; Google Cloud's Agent Platform / Amazon Bedrock / Microsoft Foundry / Claude Platform on AWS — each default off, gated behind its own CLAUDE_CODE_USE_* opt-in variable. Re-fetched and re-verified 2026-07-17; content hash unchanged from the prior retrieval.

sources.title: Claude Code settings
sources.notes: Documents the top-level env settings.json key: 'Environment variables applied to every session,' example {"FOO": "bar"}, and documents no dedicated boolean error-reporting key. Also documents the user scope resolving to ~/.claude/settings.json (lowest precedence) — the only scope this rule inspects. Re-fetched and re-verified 2026-07-17; content hash unchanged from the prior retrieval.

limitations: Only the user-level ~/.claude/settings.json env object is inspected; managed, project, local, and CLI-flag scopes are not inspected and can supply or override the effective value, and this rule cannot observe DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING set directly in the shell or process environment outside settings.json.

Default error-reporting posture is account-, version-, and provider-dependent (on only for Pro/Max sign-ins on Claude Code v2.1.198+ connecting directly to the Claude API, absent a zero-data-retention or HIPAA agreement; off in every other documented case); this rule does not determine which of these conditions hold and never infers the effective default from account or environment state.

This rule observes only the error-reporting opt-out; usage-metrics telemetry (DISABLE_TELEMETRY), the /feedback command (DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND), session quality surveys (CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY), and the broader CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC switch are separate documented controls this rule does not evaluate.

Anthropic's documentation states that known secret, file-path, and email patterns are redacted from error reports before anything leaves the machine; this rule does not independently verify that claim — no local file can.

No settings.json key named env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING appears as a worked example in either retrieved page; this rule relies on the documented, general statement that any environment variable, including DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING, may be checked into settings.json's env object. Any value other than the documented "1" opt-out token has no confirmed documented effect and is treated as unrecognized rather than assumed to enable or disable error reporting.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers and the ambient process environment are not inspected.
env.DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING set to a value other than "1" (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
